_note: Representative literature establishing each finding-#2 gene as a canonical immune regulator/target. Source: PubMed. These PMIDs become the `claimant` on the literature-vs-data Contradiction objects: the field cites these genes as central to T-cell biology; the released Marson CRISPRi data shows knockdown produces near-zero transcriptional change (they are effectors/outputs, not transcriptional drivers).

PDCD1.title: The blockade of immune checkpoints in cancer immunotherapy
PDCD1.canonical_role: PD-1: central inhibitory immune-checkpoint receptor, primary immunotherapy target

CTLA4.title: T-cell regulation by CD28 and CTLA-4
CTLA4.canonical_role: CTLA-4: key negative regulator of T-cell activation, first FDA-approved checkpoint target

HAVCR2.title: Lag-3, Tim-3, and TIGIT: Co-inhibitory Receptors with Specialized Functions in Immune Regulation
HAVCR2.canonical_role: TIM-3: co-inhibitory receptor regulating T-cell responses, clinical checkpoint target

LAG3.title: Lag-3, Tim-3, and TIGIT: Co-inhibitory Receptors with Specialized Functions in Immune Regulation
LAG3.canonical_role: LAG-3: co-inhibitory receptor, clinically validated checkpoint target

IL2.title: Regulation of Effector and Memory CD8 T Cell Differentiation by IL-2 - A Balancing Act
IL2.canonical_role: IL-2: central cytokine directing T-cell effector and memory fate

IFNG.title: CD4 T Helper Cell Subsets and Related Human Immunological Disorders
IFNG.canonical_role: IFN-gamma: defining effector cytokine of the Th1 CD4 T-cell lineage
